key_elements: Defines Order module as core feature with version 1.0.0
Declares 24 events including order lifecycle (created, updated, confirmed, preparing, ready, delivered, completed, cancelled)
Lists 10 hooks for order operations (beforeOrderCreate, afterOrderCreate, beforeStatusChange, etc.)
Defines 21 permissions across order, table, zone, qrcode, customer, feedback, service resources
Specifies 7 services: OrderService, TableService, ZoneService, QRCodeService, CustomerService, ServiceRequestService, FeedbackService
Dependencies: auth, organization, menu modules; optional: notification
Tier-based features: starter, professional, business, enterprise with different limits
Admin menu structure with 6 submenu items (Orders, Tables, Zones, QR Codes, Customers, Feedback)

file_insights.purpose: Zod validation schemas for Order module inputs/outputs
key_elements: Defines LIMITS constants for validation (name 1-100, tableNumber 1-20, notes max 500, rating 1-5, price 0-999999.99)
Enum schemas: orderStatus (7 values), orderType (3 values), orderItemStatus (4 values), tableStatus (3 values), qrCodeType (3 values), serviceRequestType (3 values)
Base schemas: cuidSchema, optionalCuidSchema, nameSchema, tableNumberSchema, notesSchema, commentSchema, messageSchema, cancelReasonSchema
Numeric schemas: quantitySchema (1-999), capacitySchema (1-50), ratingSchema (1-5), priceSchema with number/string union and validation
Zone schemas: createZoneSchema, updateZoneSchema, listZonesQuerySchema with pagination (page, perPage)
Table schemas: createTableSchema, updateTableSchema, changeTableStatusSchema, listTablesQuerySchema with sorting options
QR Code schemas: createQRCodeSchema, updateQRCodeSchema, listQRCodesQuerySchema
File truncated but shows comprehensive validation approach

file_insights.purpose: TypeScript type definitions for Order module
key_elements: File referenced in commit but full content not shown in diff

patterns_discovered.description: Order module follows modular pattern with separate concerns: module.json (metadata), order.schema.ts (validation), order.types.ts (types)
patterns_discovered.evidence: Three distinct files handling configuration, validation, and types

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive use of Zod for runtime validation with custom refinements and transforms
patterns_discovered.evidence: Price schema uses union types, transforms, and multiple refinements for robust validation

patterns_discovered.description: Status and type enums define valid states throughout the module
patterns_discovered.evidence: orderStatus, orderType, orderItemStatus, tableStatus, qrCodeType, serviceRequestTypeSchema

patterns_discovered.description: List query schemas include pagination (page, perPage), search, and filtering capabilities
patterns_discovered.evidence: listZonesQuerySchema, listTablesQuerySchema, listQRCodesQuerySchema all support pagination

patterns_discovered.description: 24 events defined for order lifecycle and related operations
patterns_discovered.evidence: module.json provides comprehensive event listing across order, table, zone, qrcode, customer, service, and feedback

patterns_discovered.description: Features restricted by subscription tier (starter, professional, business, enterprise)
patterns_discovered.evidence: Tier definitions in module.json with different feature sets and limits per tier

patterns_discovered.description: 10 hooks defined for custom logic injection at critical points
Fine-grained permissions for resource operations (read, create, update, delete, manage)
patterns_discovered.evidence: 21 distinct permissions across order, table, zone, qrcode, customer, feedback, service

gotchas_discovered.description: Distinction between .optional() and .nullable() in schemas - optionalCuidSchema uses both
gotchas_discovered.impact: May cause confusion about whether null vs undefined is expected

gotchas_discovered.description: Price schema accepts string or number and converts to float with validation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could mask precision issues if not carefully handled in database layer
gotchas_discovered.example: priceSchema uses union with transform - needs consistent decimal handling

gotchas_discovered.description: hexColorSchema is optional AND nullable, may lead to three different states (undefined, null, valid hex)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Frontend and database handling must account for all three states

gotchas_discovered.description: order.types.ts file not shown in diff, and order.schema.ts appears truncated
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot verify complete schema definitions or type definitions structure
gotchas_discovered.example: File truncated mid-definition in git diff output

gotchas_discovered.description: listTablesQuerySchema defaults to 'sortOrder' sorting but doesn't specify default sort order direction clearly
gotchas_discovered.impact: Default behavior may not match user expectations
gotchas_discovered.example: sortBy defaults to 'sortOrder', sortOrder defaults to 'asc'

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created Order module foundation with comprehensive structure, validation schemas, and configuration
key_achievements: Established module metadata in module.json with clear dependencies, permissions, events, and tier definitions
Created extensive Zod validation schemas covering all major entities (zones, tables, QR codes, and more)
Implemented constants-based validation limits for maintainability
Defined enum schemas for status and type enumerations
Created CRUD operation schemas with input/output types
Included pagination and filtering support in list operations
Established permission and event architecture for module integration
approach_outcome.notes: Subtask completed as single atomic commit. Module structure follows established patterns from other modules in the codebase.

recommendations.recommendation: Clarify distinction between optional and nullable fields in documentation - create helper types for common patterns
recommendations.rationale: Three-state logic (undefined/null/value) can lead to subtle bugs if not explicitly handled

recommendations.recommendation: Add JSDoc examples for complex schemas like priceSchema showing string and number input handling
recommendations.rationale: Type coercion patterns may not be obvious to consumers of the schema library

recommendations.recommendation: Create reusable schema patterns/builders for pagination and filtering to reduce duplication
recommendations.rationale: listZonesQuerySchema, listTablesQuerySchema, listQRCodesQuerySchema share similar structure

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive test suite for all schemas, especially edge cases like price boundary values and color format validation
recommendations.rationale: Validation schemas are critical for data integrity and deserve thorough test coverage

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding query optimization hints in listQRCodesQuerySchema regarding isActive string-to-boolean transformation
recommendations.rationale: String parsing on every query could impact performance with large datasets

recommendations.recommendation: Export LIMITS constant for use in other modules that need to reference validation constraints
recommendations.rationale: Currently only internal - external modules may need to know max values for UI hints

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding discriminated union schemas for different order types (DINE_IN, TAKEAWAY, DELIVERY) with type-specific fields
recommendations.rationale: Different order types likely have different field requirements that could be better expressed with unions
